mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a healthy and robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, low unemployment correlates with economic expansion.
regime_hint: any regime with stable labor market
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 5.5% for 6 consecutive months, yet S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a critical threshold, it signals increasing inflationary pressures, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations by raising discount rates and reducing future earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflationary spikes often precede Fed rate hikes.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently exceeds 3.0% YoY, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with consistent job creation (PAYEMS) indicates strong consumer demand and a healthy economic expansion, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1%
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and PAYEMS show sustained growth above their respective thresholds for 3 consecutive months, yet S&P 500 20-day returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction or slowdown, a sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic demand, leading to reduced corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% on average over 3 months during a confirmed contraction, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, often indicative of an impending or ongoing recession, which severely impacts corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Sharp increases in unemployment are historically associated with recessions.
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer confidence and investment in real estate, indicating underlying economic strength that supports broader market performance and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market strength often precedes broader economic growth.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a positive 3-month average growth rate for 6 consecutive months, yet S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: A low Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an accommodative monetary environment that fuels consumer spending, boosting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates encourage borrowing and spending.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0% and RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, strong industrial production (INDPRO) growth signifies healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating strong corporate output and profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key indicator of economic supply-side health.
failure_condition: If INDPRO consistently shows growth above 0.5% on average over 3 months during an expansion, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods.
